name: Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop
name_original: Desde Nerja a Frigiliana y Torrox – circular por EuroVelo 8
description: Cycle a difficult 20.4-mile loop from Nerja to Frigiliana and Torrox via EuroVelo 8, featuring sea views and white villages.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Nerja features a varied landscape, from the Mediterranean coastline with its cliffs and coves to the rugged Sierra de Almijara mountains. The region includes river valleys like Río Chíllar, alongside fruit valleys, olive groves, and vineyards. This topography provides a range of routes, from coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Nerja The most popular touring cycling route is Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through the countryside, connecting charming white villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lagos Beach – Monument to Peace loop from Nerja, a difficult 37.2 miles (59.9 km) path. This route provides extensive coastal views and passes by significant landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Maro Aqueduct – La Herradura Beach loop from Nerja, a 39.7 miles (63.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past historical structures like the Eagle Aqueduct, often completed in about 4 hours 27 minutes. Touring cycling around Nerja is defined by rugged mountains, coastal paths, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Nerja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nerja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Nerja?
A: Nerja offers a diverse network of nearly 40 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 5 easy, 13 moderate, and 21 difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nerja?
A: The mild, year-round climate in Nerja, with over 320 sunny days annually, makes it suitable for cycling almost anytime. However, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are particularly recommended due to the ideal weather conditions.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Nerja?
A: Yes, Nerja has 5 easy touring cycling routes that are great for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes often feature gentler terrain and less elevation, allowing for an enjoyable experience for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Nerja?
A: Touring cycling around Nerja offers a varied landscape. You'll find routes along the Mediterranean coastline with spectacular cliffs and coves, through river valleys like Río Chíllar, and into the rugged Sierra de Almijara mountains. Expect a mix of fruit valleys, olive groves, and vineyards, providing diverse and picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in the Nerja area?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Nerja are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981456115/nerja-frigiliana-and-torrox-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nerja, Frigiliana, and Torrox Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that takes you through charming white villages and countryside.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation gains?
A: Absolutely. Nerja is nestled against the Sierra de Almijara mountains, offering 21 difficult touring cycling routes with substantial elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19342983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alberquillas Beach – View of the Maro Aqueduct loop from Nerja&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1,300 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride with rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Which touring cycling routes offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Nerja boast stunning vistas. Coastal rides often provide breathtaking sea views, while mountain routes offer panoramic glimpses of the countryside and the Mediterranean. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21836898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Peñoncillo Viewpoint – Balcony of the Mediterranean loop from Nerja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed to showcase scenic coastal viewpoints.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass by historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical and cultural points of interest. You can cycle through charming white villages like Frigiliana, known for its Moorish influence. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Maro Aqueduct – La Herradura Beach loop from Nerja&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5427107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eagle Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, an early 19th-century structure.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that provide access to beaches or wild swimming spots?
A: The coastline around Nerja is dotted with beautiful beaches and hidden coves. Routes along the Río Chíllar connect to various beaches, and the Paraje Natural de los Acantilados de Maro-Cerro Gordo offers stunning coastal cycling experiences with access to crystal-clear waters for swimming.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Nerja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nerja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2,500 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic coastal paths.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that explore the Sierra de Almijara mountains?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Almijara, Tejeda, and Alhama Natural Park provides numerous opportunities for touring cyclists. These routes offer challenging ascents and descents, rewarding riders with spectacular panoramic views of the region. The Pico del Cielo, at 1,505 meters, is a prominent feature in this mountainous landscape.

Q: Can I cycle through charming white villages in the Nerja region?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its picturesque 'white villages.' Routes like the one connecting Nerja to Frigiliana and Cómpeta allow you to experience the unique beauty and Moorish charm of these traditional Andalusian settlements.
tips:
The white mountain village of Frigiliana on the southern foothills of the Sierra Almijara has been voted the most beautiful village in Andalusia several times.
Since the Neolithic Age and around 3,000 BC, the surrounding region has also been inhabited mainly in caves by the inhabitants of that time known. A mighty menhir north-east of the city center of Frigiliana provides information about this.

Source and more info
https://www.andalusien360.de/frigiliana

Torrox is a charming town worth seeing and is part of the Axarquía region. The town is located east of Málaga and offers cultural diversity, a pleasant Mediterranean climate and a beautiful coastline. Torrox is a wonderful touring destination for those who want to experience the other side of the Costa del Sol as well as traditional Andalusian culture, while also having access to wonderful beaches and further cycling routes.

The picturesque old village of Torrox Pueblo on the hill offers a charming contrast to the modern coastal area. The Plaza de las Américas is a central meeting point with cafes and shops.
Very nice mirador that offers a beautiful view of the cliffs and the small beaches. Wonderful views, a great experience! Just take a short stop and capture the perspectives.

On the steep coast you can find this rock, on which a white miniature chapel stands. A few niches have been carved into the rock, in which, among other things, a picture of the Virgin del Carmen (patron saint of fishermen and sailors) is kept. People put flowers there, light candles and place small, inscribed stones on the rock. With the sea in the background and the view of the coast, it is a real eye-catcher. A point of attraction, it was very well attended.

This 15th century conical watchtower stands at a height of about 40 metres and is clearly visible from the road. The tower is located on private, fenced, inaccessible land, so it can only be viewed from below.

A quieter and lesser-known stretch of coast around Nerja. This region is characterized by natural coves, often located away from the main beaches and less frequented. The beach consists of pebbles and sand, surrounded by rocks. The nearby river (although usually dry) shapes the surrounding area and provides a beautiful backdrop.

faqs.question: How challenging is the cycling route from Nerja to Frigiliana and Torrox?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult, requiring very good fitness. It features significant climbing, particularly in the first part of the ride, with some sections being steep. While most surfaces are paved, be prepared for a demanding ascent before enjoying a rapid descent towards Torrox Costa and a flatter return to Nerja.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along this EuroVelo 8 section?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular and varied scenery. The route offers incredible views over the Mediterranean Sea, with the possibility of seeing the Atlas Mountains of Morocco on clear days. You'll traverse mountainous landscapes, green-hilled valleys, and pass through charming 'white villages' like Frigiliana White Village and Plaza de las Américas, Torrox.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 32.9 km (20.5 miles) long with an estimated cycling duration of around 2 hours and 22 minutes. However, due to its difficult rating and significant elevation gain, allow extra time for stops, enjoying the views, and navigating the challenging sections.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the picturesque Frigiliana White Village, known for its Múdejar neighborhood, and Plaza de las Américas, Torrox. You'll also pass by scenic spots like El Peñoncillo Viewpoint, the Virgen del Mar Memorial, and the Torre de Calaceite, Barco de Arroz Dive. The route also offers glimpses of Playa Casa Blanca seen from Sanantona.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this cycling route in Nerja?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Nerja offers various public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking near the starting point of the EuroVelo 8 section in Nerja, keeping in mind that it's a popular coastal town.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa del Sol region, including Nerja and Torrox, is known for having a pleasant climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally ideal, offering comfortable temperatures for cycling and avoiding the intense heat of summer. Winter can also be pleasant, though days are shorter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route, which includes sections of the EuroVelo 8. You are free to enjoy the trail at your leisure.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant climbing, and steep sections, this route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires very good fitness and experience with challenging ascents and descents. More moderate road ride variants might exist in the area, but this specific circular route is demanding.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers parts of Valle de Frigiliana (27%), La Senda Litoral (16%), and Torrox - Nerja (16%). You'll also find smaller segments of Ruta Acebuchal, Ruta Venta Cebolleros, and Ruta La Lastra, among others.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, this specific route is a challenging touring bicycle trail with significant road sections and elevation changes. Cycling with dogs, especially on such demanding routes, can be difficult and potentially unsafe for both the rider and the dog. It's generally not recommended for this type of activity.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for tackling the challenging climb on this route?
faqs.answer: The climbing on this route is 'front-loaded and steep in parts,' especially as you head towards Frigiliana. A strategic tip is to conserve energy during the initial ascent and ensure your bike is in good climbing condition. The reward is a rapid descent towards Torrox Costa and a more relaxed coastal return to Nerja, so pace yourself for the initial effort.

waypoints.name: Torre de Calaceite, Barco de Arroz Dive
Playa Casa Blanca seen from Sanantona
